feat: checkpoint resume support (#3744) (#65)

The runner can now save checkpoints after each step and resume from them:

1. Enable with: runner.with_checkpoints(true)
2. After each successful step, a checkpoint JSON file is saved to
   /tmp/amplihack-checkpoint-{name}-{pid}.json containing:
   - List of completed step IDs
   - Full context state
   - Timestamp
3. Resume with: runner.with_resume_from(&checkpoint)
   - Steps already in the checkpoint are skipped
   - Execution continues from the first non-completed step
   - Context is restored from the checkpoint

This means a 2-hour workflow that fails at step 15 can be resumed
from step 15 instead of starting over from scratch.

Also adds RecipeCheckpoint model with save/load methods and
a data() accessor on RecipeContext.

Bumps to 0.3.0 (semver minor — new public API). All 467 tests pass.

Closes amplihack #3744

commit.message:
feat: per-step timeout support for agent steps (#3743) (#64)

The execute_agent_step adapter interface now accepts an optional timeout
parameter. Recipe YAML steps can declare timeout: 600 (seconds) for
agent steps, matching the existing bash step timeout support.

The timeout parameter is plumbed through the full stack:
- Adapter trait interface
- CLISubprocessAdapter implementation
- RecipeRunner step execution
- Recovery and retry agent calls

Note: actual timeout enforcement for agent steps is stubbed (agent
steps are long-running by nature; enforcing requires careful cleanup
of partial output). The interface is ready for enforcement in a
follow-up.

Also: native structured log file (v0.2.13), agent output resilience
(v0.2.11), and context schema validation (v0.2.12) are included.

Bumps to 0.2.14. All 284 tests pass.

Closes amplihack #3743

commit.message:
feat: native structured log file owned by Rust binary (#3742) (#63)

The runner now creates a persistent JSON log file when --progress is set:
/tmp/amplihack-recipe-{name}-{pid}.log

Each line is a self-contained JSON event:
- recipe_start: recipe name, pid, timestamp
- step_transition: step start/done/fail/skip with duration
- output: agent/bash output lines

The FileLogListener also prints to stderr (replacing StderrListener)
so live progress continues to work. Falls back to StderrListener if
file creation fails.

Callers can tail -f the log file for real-time structured progress.
Replaces the Python-side log wrapper from PR #3712.

Bumps to 0.2.13. All 95 tests pass.

Closes amplihack #3742

commit.message:
fix: retry and graceful fallback for missing agent output files (#3740) (#61)

When the agent output file is missing after step execution:
1. Retry once after 1s (handles transient filesystem delays)
2. If still missing, log error with the expected path
3. Continue with empty output instead of aborting

Previously, a missing output file crashed the entire workflow with
'Failed to read agent output file: No such file or directory'.
This lost hours of work from prior completed steps.

Bumps version to 0.2.11. All 95 tests pass.

Closes amplihack #3740, #3711

commit.message:
fix: pass context via JSON file when env vars exceed OS limit (#3738) (#58)

When accumulated context (after parallel workstreams) exceeds ~1.5MB,
the runner now writes context to a temp JSON file and passes
AMPLIHACK_CONTEXT_FILE env var instead of individual RECIPE_VAR_* vars.

This prevents the 'Argument list too long' (E2BIG) error that crashed
cleanup and reflection steps after parallel execution.

Behavior:
- Context < 1.5MB: RECIPE_VAR_* env vars (backward compatible)
- Context >= 1.5MB: AMPLIHACK_CONTEXT_FILE + critical vars only
  (task_description, repo_path, task_type, workstream_count)
- Temp file has 0600 permissions, cleaned up after each step
- Template {{var}} expansion in YAML still works (pre-rendered)

All 95 tests pass.

Closes #3738

commit.message:
fix(veml6040): align init I2C writes with reference driver and add robust retry

- Match reference MicroPython VEML6040 init behavior by writing one-byte
  config values at register 0x00 (shutdown then default)
- Check writeRegister return codes in initialize() so I2C write failures are
  not silently ignored
- Track init readiness and re-initialize the singleton on subsequent API
  calls when initialization previously failed, preventing permanent stuck
  all-zero readings after transient startup bus errors

This addresses field reports of persistent RGBW=0/None/Hue=0/Sat=0 despite
working hardware by removing silent init failure paths and ensuring retries.

fix(veml6040): fix null colour returns in block UI

Three bugs caused classifyColor() to always return null/mismatch in the
MakeCode block interface:

1. classifyColor() returned string ('red', 'none') while the block UI
   comparison dropdown uses the ColorName enum (integers). Any 'if colour
   = red' block comparison silently evaluated to false. Changed return
   type to ColorName enum so both sides of the comparison are the same
   type.

2. Three blockId values were duplicated between class methods and their
   namespace-level wrapper functions (veml6040_classify_color,
   veml6040_ambient_light, veml6040_color_temperature). PXT/MakeCode
   misbehaves with duplicate blockIds. Removed all block annotations
   from class methods — the wrapper functions are the correct block entry
   points.

3. CONFIG register (16-bit) was written with a 1-byte data buffer instead
   of 2 bytes. The missing high byte may cause a silent I2C failure,
   leaving the sensor in shutdown. Fixed to match the VEML6030 pattern.

Also added ColorName.White and ColorName.Black enum values (appended after
None to preserve existing ordinals) since classifyColor() can detect both.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Phase 5b-c: Paint absorption model and capillary flow

Add two new simulation passes between diffusion and drying:

- Absorption: canvas absorbs thin wet paint over time, reducing wetness
  and making paint permanent (staining effect for glazes/washes)
- Capillary flow: paint flows preferentially along canvas grooves via
  paper texture gradient, settling in valleys with texture-aware diffusion

New medium physics parameters (absorptionRate, capillaryStrength) added
with per-medium defaults: Oil (slow), Acrylic (moderate), Watercolor (fast).

Phase 1: Navier-Stokes fluid solver with pressure projection, surface tension, and vorticity

- New wet_media_pressure.wgsl: Jacobi iterative pressure solver with
  divergence computation and gradient subtraction for incompressible flow
- New wet_media_surface_tension.wgsl: Curvature-based surface tension
  with edge accumulation (bead-up effect) for realistic oil/acrylic edges
- Enhanced wet_media_advect.wgsl: Added vorticity confinement to preserve
  swirl detail, gravity-driven flow, and viscosity-aware advection speed

  → Surface Tension → Advect → Diffuse → Dry

  vorticityStrength, gravityStrength, and pressureIterations

commit.message:
feat: implement all four @nestjs-stitcher packages

Extract GraphQL schema stitching engine from poll-app into reusable
NestJS modules organized as four packages:

- @nestjs-stitcher/common: HMAC utilities, config tokens, error classes,
  shared types (StitcherUser, TrustedRequestExtensions)
- @nestjs-stitcher/auth: JWT authentication with remote JWKS and local
  key strategies, guards (StitcherAuthGuard, RolesGuard), decorators
  (@Public, @Roles, @CurrentUser), AuthModule.forRoot()
- @nestjs-stitcher/subgraph: Federation helpers (prepareSchemaForFederation),
  HMAC validation interceptor, bootstrap utilities, SubgraphModule.forRoot()
- @nestjs-stitcher/gateway: Schema stitcher with reactive RxJS composition,
  endpoint loaders (Local, DNS), executor factory with extension visitor
  pipeline, GatewayModule.forRoot()

Key architectural decisions:
- Pure NestJS guards (replaces passport)
- jose library for JWT verification (replaces passport-jwt)
- Native fetch (replaces @whatwg-node/fetch)
- setInterval for auto-reload (replaces @nestjs/schedule)
- Native JS utilities (replaces lodash)

Tooling: Vitest, Biome, SWC, pnpm workspaces, Nx
All 29 tests passing across 10 test files.
